Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999, to make related amendments to the Food and Drugs Act and to repeal the Perfluorooctane Sulfonate Virtual Elimination Act
Identification.ShortTitle: Strengthening Environmental Protection for a Healthier Canada Act
Identification.RunningHead: Strengthening Environmental Protection for a Healthier Canada Act

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 to, among other things,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: recognize that every individual in Canada has a right to a healthy environment as provided under that Act;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: provide that the Government of Canada must protect that right as provided under that Act, and, in doing so, may balance that right with relevant factors;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: require the development of an implementation framework that sets out how that right will be considered in the administration of that Act, and require that research, studies or monitoring activities be conducted to support the Government of Canada in protecting that right;

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: authorize the Minister of the Environment to add to the Domestic Substances List certain substances that were in commerce in Canada and subject to the Food and Drugs Act between January 1, 1987 and September 13, 2001, and provide that any substance may be deleted from the List when it is no longer in commerce in Canada;

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: require that the Minister of the Environment and the Minister of Health develop a plan that specifies the substances to which those Ministers are satisfied priority should be given in assessing whether they are toxic or capable of becoming toxic;

Provision.Label: (f)
Provision.Text: provide that any person may request that those Ministers assess a substance;

Provision.Label: (g)
Provision.Text: require the Minister of the Environment to compile a list of substances that that Minister and the Minister of Health have reason to suspect are capable of becoming toxic or that have been determined to be capable of becoming toxic;

Provision.Label: (h)
Provision.Text: require that, when those Ministers conduct or interpret the results of certain assessments â or conduct or interpret the results of a review of decisions of certain governments â in order to determine whether a substance is toxic or capable of becoming toxic, they consider available information on whether there is a vulnerable population in relation to the substance and on the cumulative effects that may result from exposure to the substance in combination with exposure to other substances;

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: provide that certain substances be classified as substances that pose the highest risk based on, among other things, their properties or characteristics;

Provision.Label: (j)
Provision.Text: require that those Ministers give priority to the total, partial or conditional prohibition of activities in relation to toxic substances that are specified in Part 1 of Schedule 1 of the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999, or to the total, partial or conditional prohibition of releases of those substances into the environment, when regulations or instruments respecting preventive or control actions in relation to those substances are developed;

Provision.Label: (k)
Provision.Text: expand certain regulation-making, information-gathering and pollution prevention powers under that Act, including by adding a reference to products that may release substances into the environment;

Provision.Label: (l)
Provision.Text: allow the risks associated with certain toxic substances to be managed by preventive or control actions taken under any other Act of Parliament, and the obligations under sections 91 and 92 of the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 to be the responsibility of whoever of the Minister of the Environment or the Minister of Health is best placed to fulfil them;

Provision.Label: (m)
Provision.Text: expand the powers of the Minister of the Environment to vary either the contents of a significant new activity notice with respect to a substance not on the Domestic Substances List or the contents of the List itself with respect to a substance on the List that is subject to the significant new activities provisions of that Act;

Provision.Label: (n)
Provision.Text: extend the requirement, to notify persons of the obligation to comply with the significant new activity provisions of that Act when a substance that is subject to those provisions is transferred to them, so that it applies with respect to substances on the Domestic Substances List, and authorize that Minister to limit by class the persons who are required to be notified of the obligation when a substance that is subject to those provisions is transferred to them; and

Provision.Label: (o)
Provision.Text: require that confidentiality requests made under section 313 of the Act be accompanied by reasons, and to allow the Minister of the Environment to disclose the explicit chemical or biological name of a substance or the explicit biological name of a living organism in certain circumstances.

The enactment also makes related amendments to the Food and Drugs Act to enable the assessment and management of risks to the environment associated with foods, drugs, cosmetics and devices by, among other things,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: prohibiting persons from conducting certain activities in respect of a drug unless the Minister of Health has conducted an assessment of the risks to the environment presented by certain substances contained in that drug;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: enabling the Minister of Health to take measures in respect of the risks to the environment that a drug may present throughout its life cycle; and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: providing the Governor in Council with supporting regulation-making authorities.
Finally, the enactment repeals the Perfluorooctane Sulfonate Virtual Elimination Act.

Heading.TitleText: An Act to amend the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999, to make related amendments to the Food and Drugs Act and to repeal the Perfluorooctane Sulfonate Virtual Elimination Act

Text.XRefExternal: Strengthening Environmental Protection for a Healthier Canada Act

Heading.TitleText: Related Amendments to the Food and Drugs Act

Provision.Text: Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Strengthening Environmental Protection for a Healthier Canada Act.

Subsection.Text: The preamble to the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 is amended by adding the following after the first paragraph:

Text.Ins: Whereas the Government of Canada recognizes that every individual in Canada has a right to a healthy environment as provided under this Act;

Subsection.Text: The third paragraph of the preamble to the Act is replaced by the following:

Provision.Text: Whereas the Government of Canada acknowledges the need to control and manage pollutants and wastes if their release into the environment cannot be prevented;

ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Existing text of the third paragraph of the preamble:

Provision.Text: Whereas the Government of Canada acknowledges the need to virtually eliminate the most persistent and bioaccumulative toxic substances and the need to control and manage pollutants and wastes if their release into the environment cannot be prevented;

Subsection.Text: The preamble to the Act is amended by adding the following after the eighth paragraph:

Text.Ins: Whereas the Government of Canada is committed to implementing the United Nations Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples;

Subsection.Text: The preamble to the Act is amended by adding the following after the ninth paragraph:

Provision.Text: Whereas the Government of Canada recognizes the importance of considering vulnerable populations in assessing whether substances are toxic or capable of becoming toxic;
Whereas the Government of Canada recognizes the importance of minimizing the risks posed by exposure to toxic substances and the cumulative effects of toxic substances;

Subsection.Text: The preamble to the Act is amended by adding the following after the 10th paragraph:

Text.Ins: Whereas the Government of Canada recognizes the role of science in the process of making decisions related to the protection of the environment and human health, as well as the importance of promoting the development and timely incorporation of scientifically justified alternative methods and strategies in the testing and assessment of substances to reduce, refine or replace the use of vertebrate animals;

Subsection.Text: The 13th paragraph of the preamble to the Act is replaced by the following:

Provision.Text: Whereas the Government of Canada will endeavour to remove threats to biological diversity through pollution prevention as well as the control and management of the risk of any adverse effects of the use and release of toxic substances, pollutants and wastes;

Whereas the Government of Canada recognizes the importance of encouraging the progressive substitution of substances, processes and technologies with alternatives that are safer for the environment or human health, when they are economically and technically viable;

Whereas the Government of Canada recognizes the importance of Canadians having information, including by means of the packaging and labelling of products, regarding the risks posed by toxic substances to the environment or to human health;

ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Existing text of the 13th paragraph of the preamble:

Provision.Text: Whereas the Government of Canada will endeavour to remove threats to biological diversity through pollution prevention, the control and management of the risk of any adverse effects of the use and release of toxic substances, pollutants and wastes, and the virtual elimination of persistent and bioaccumulative toxic substances;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 2(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: exercise its powers in a manner that

Subparagraph.Text: protects the environment and human health, including the health of vulnerable populations,

applies the precautionary principle, which provides that the lack of full scientific certainty shall not be used as a reason for postponing cost-effective measures to prevent environmental degradation if there are threats of serious or irreversible damage, and
promotes and reinforces enforceable pollution prevention approaches;

Subsection.Text: In the administration of this Act, the Government of Canada shall, having regard to the Constitution and laws of Canada and subject to subsection (1.1),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: exercise its powers in a manner that protects the environment and human health, applies the precautionary principle that, where there are threats of serious or irreversible damage, lack of full scientific certainty shall not be used as a reason for postponing cost-effective measures to prevent environmental degradation, and promotes and reinforces enforceable pollution prevention approaches;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 2(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a.1):

Paragraph.Label: (a.2)
Paragraph.Text: protect the right of every individual in Canada to a healthy environment as provided under this Act, which right may be balanced with relevant factors, including social, economic, health and scientific factors;

Subsection.Text: The portion of the definition substance in subsection 3(1) of the Act after paragraph (d) and before paragraph (e) is replaced by the following:

ContinuedDefinition.Text: and, except for the purposes of sections 66 to 66.2, 80 to 89 and 104 to 115, includes

Definition.Text: substanceâmeans any distinguishable kind of organic or inorganic matter, whether animate or inanimate, and includes

Paragraph.Text: Â

ContinuedDefinition.Text: and, except for the purposes of sections 66, 80 to 89 and 104 to 115, includes

Subsection.Text: Subsection 3(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: vulnerable population means a group of individuals within the Canadian population who, due to greater susceptibility or greater exposure, may be at an increased risk of experiencing adverse health effects from exposure to substances. (population vulnÃ©rable)

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after the heading of Part 1:

Heading.TitleText: Implementation of Right to a Healthy Environment

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of paragraph 2(1)(a.2), the Ministers shall, within two years after the day on which this section comes into force, develop an implementation framework to set out how the right to a healthy environment will be considered in the administration of this Act.
The implementation framework shall, among other things, elaborate on

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the principles to be considered in the administration of this Act, such as principles of environmental justice â including the avoidance of adverse effects that disproportionately affect vulnerable populations â and the principle of non-regression;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: research, studies or monitoring activities to support the protection of the right to a healthy environment referred to in paragraph 2(1)(a.2); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the balancing of that right with relevant factors, including social, economic, health and scientific factors.

Subsection.Text: In developing the implementation framework, the Ministers shall consult any interested persons.
The Minister shall publish the implementation framework in the manner that the Minister considers appropriate.
The Minister shall include in the annual report required by section 342 a report on the implementation of the framework.

Section.Text: Section 15 of the Act is replaced by the following:

The rights conferred by this Part are in addition to the right to request, under section 76, that a substance be assessed, the right to file a notice of objection under Parts 1, 7 and 11 and the right to request under Parts 7 and 11 that a board of review be established under section 333.

The rights conferred by this Part are in addition to the right to request the addition of a substance to the Priority Substance List, the right to file a notice of objection under Parts 1, 5, 7 and 11 and the right to request under Parts 5, 7 and 11 that a board of review be established under section 333.
Section 44 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Protection of right to healthy environment
Subsection.Text: The Ministers shall conduct research, studies or monitoring activities to support the Government of Canada in protecting the right to a healthy environment referred to in paragraph 2(1)(a.2).
Paragraph 45(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: conduct research and studies, including biomonitoring surveys, relating to the role of substances in illnesses or in health problems;

Section.Text: The Minister of Health shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: conduct research and studies relating to the role of substances in illnesses or in health problems;

Subsection.Text: Section 45 of the Act is renumbered as subsection 45(1) and is amended by adding the following:
For greater certainty, the research and studies referred to in paragraph (1)(a) may relate to vulnerable populations.
Paragraph 46(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: substances specified in the plan developed under section 73;

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, for the purpose of conducting research, creating an inventory of data, formulating objectives and codes of practice, issuing guidelines or assessing or reporting on the state of the environment, publish in the Canada Gazette and in any other manner that the Minister considers appropriate a notice requiring any person described in the notice to provide the Minister with any information that may be in the possession of that person or to which the person may reasonably be expected to have access, including information regarding the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: substances on the Priority Substances List;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 46(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (e):

Paragraph.Label: (e.1)
Paragraph.Text: products that contain a substance that is toxic under section 64 or that may become toxic, or products that may release such a substance into the environment;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 46(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (k):

Text.Ins: activities that may contribute to pollution;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 56(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Minister may, at any time, publish in the Canada Gazette and in any other manner that the Minister considers appropriate a notice requiring any person or class of persons described in the notice to prepare and implement a pollution prevention plan in respect of

Paragraph.Text: a substance or group of substances specified on the list of toxic substances in Schedule 1;
a substance or group of substances with respect to which subsection 166(1) or 176(1) applies; or

Text.Ins: a product that contains a substance specified on the list of toxic substances in Schedule 1 or that may release such a substance into the environment.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, at any time, publish in the Canada Gazette and in any other manner that the Minister considers appropriate a notice requiring any person or class of persons described in the notice to prepare and implement a pollution prevention plan in respect of a substance or group of substances specified on the List of Toxic Substances in Schedule 1, or to which subsection 166(1) or 176(1) applies.
Paragraph 56(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the substance, group of substances or product in relation to which the plan is to be prepared;

Subsection.Text: The notice may specify

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the substance or group of substances in relation to which the plan is to be prepared;

Section.Text: Subsection 60(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Minister may publish in the Canada Gazette and in any other manner that the Minister considers appropriate a notice requiring any person or class of persons described in the notice who are required to prepare and implement a pollution prevention plan under section 56 to submit, within the period specified by the Minister, the plan or any part of the plan for the purpose of determining and assessing preventive or control actions in respect of a substance, group of substances or product.

The Minister may publish in the Canada Gazette and in any other manner that the Minister considers appropriate a notice requiring any person or class of persons described in the notice who are required to prepare and implement a pollution prevention plan under section 56 to submit, within the period specified by the Minister, the plan or any part of the plan for the purpose of determining and assessing preventive or control actions in respect of a substance or group of substances.

Section.Text: Sections 65 and 65.1 of the Act are repealed.

Subsection.Text: In this Part, virtual elimination means, in respect of a toxic substance released into the environment as a result of human activity, the ultimate reduction of the quantity or concentration of the substance in the release below the level of quantification specified by the Ministers in the List referred to in subsection (2).
The Ministers shall compile a list to be known as the Virtual Elimination List, and the List shall specify the level of quantification for each substance on the List.

When the level of quantification for a substance has been specified on the List referred to in subsection (2), the Ministers shall prescribe the quantity or concentration of the substance that may be released into the environment either alone or in combination with any other substance from any source or type of source, and, in doing so, shall take into account any factor or information provided for in section 91, including environmental or health risks and any other relevant social, economic or technical matters.

Section.Text: In section 65, level of quantification means, in respect of a substance, the lowest concentration that can be accurately measured using sensitive but routine sampling and analytical methods.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 66(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

The Minister shall, for the purpose of section 81, maintain a list to be known as the Domestic Substances List and, subject to subsection 66.2(1), the List shall specify all substances that the Minister is satisfied were, between January 1, 1984 and December 31, 1986,

The Minister shall, for the purposes of sections 73, 74 and 81, maintain a list to be known as the Domestic Substances List, and the List shall specify all substances that the Minister is satisfied were, between January 1, 1984 and December 31, 1986,
Paragraph 66(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the substances referred to in subsection (1), unless the substance has been deleted from the Domestic Substances List under subsection 66.2(1); and

Subsection.Text: The Minister shall, for the purpose of section 81, maintain a list to be known as the Non-domestic Substances List, and the List shall specify substances, other than

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the substances referred to in subsection (1); and

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 66:

Section.MarginalNote: Domestic Substances List â Food and Drugs Act

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, for the purpose of section 81, add to the Domestic Substances List any substance if the Minister is satisfied that, between January 1, 1987 and September 13, 2001, the substance

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: was in Canadian commerce as a product to which the Food and Drugs Act applied; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: was in Canadian commerce as a substance that was in or on such a product.

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: If the substance appears on the Non-domestic Substances List, the Minister shall delete it from that List.

Subsection.Text: If the Minister includes a substance on the Domestic Substances List and subsequently learns that, between January 1, 1987 and September 13, 2001, the requirements set out in paragraph (1)(a) or (b) were not met in respect of the substance, the Minister shall delete the substance from the List and may add it to the Non-domestic Substances List.
The Minister may, by order, designate any person or class of persons to exercise the powers and perform the duties and functions set out in this section.

If the Minister is satisfied that a substance referred to in subsection 66(1) that is specified on the Domestic Substances List â or a substance added to the List under subsection 66.1(1) or 87(1) or (5) â is not being manufactured in Canada, imported into Canada, in Canadian commerce or used for commercial manufacturing purposes in Canada, the Minister may delete the substance from the List and may add it to the Non-domestic Substances List.

Before deleting a substance from the Domestic Substances List under subsection (1), the Minister shall publish in the Canada Gazette and in any other manner that the Minister considers appropriate a notice indicating the Ministerâs intention to delete it from the List.
Within 60 days after the publication of the notice, any person may file written comments with the Minister.
The Minister may, by order, designate any person or class of persons to exercise the powers and perform the duties and functions set out in this section.
Paragraph 67(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting a property or characteristic of a substance, including persistence, bioaccumulation, carcinogenicity, mutagenicity and reproductive toxicity;

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Ministers, make regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting a property or characteristic of a substance, including, without limiting the generality of the foregoing, persistence and bioaccumulation;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 67(1) of the Act is amended by striking out âandâ at the end of paragraph (c), by adding âandâ at the end of paragraph (d) and by adding the following after paragraph (d):

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: respecting, for the purpose of subsection 77(3), the classification of a substance as a substance that poses the highest risk.

Subsection.Text: The portion of section 68 of the Act before subparagraph (a)(i) is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: For the purpose of assessing whether a substance is toxic or is capable of becoming toxic â or for the purpose of assessing whether to control, or the manner in which to control, a substance, a product that contains a substance or a product that may release a substance into the environment â including a substance specified on the list of toxic substances in Schedule 1, either Minister may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: collect or generate data and conduct investigations respecting any matter in relation to the substance or product including

Section.Text: For the purpose of assessing whether a substance is toxic or is capable of becoming toxic, or for the purpose of assessing whether to control, or the manner in which to control, a substance, including a substance specified on the List of Toxic Substances in Schedule 1, either Minister may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: collect or generate data and conduct investigations respecting any matter in relation to a substance, including, without limiting the generality of the foregoing,

Subparagraph.Text: Â

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: the ability of the substance to cause delayed or latent effects over the lifetime of an organism,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: the ability of the substance to cause reproductive or survival impairment of an organism,
Â

Subparagraph.Label: (xii)
Subparagraph.Text: the development and use of alternatives to the substance,
Â

Paragraph.Text: Â

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: provide information and make recommendations respecting any matter in relation to a substance, including, without limiting the generality of the foregoing, measures to control the presence of the substance in the environment.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 68(a) of the Act is amended by adding the following after subparagraph (iii):

Subparagraph.Label: (iii.1)
Subparagraph.Text: whether exposure to the substance in combination with exposure to other substances has the potential to cause cumulative effects,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii.2)
Subparagraph.Text: whether there is a vulnerable population in relation to the substance,

Subsection.Text: Subparagraphs 68(a)(v) and (vi) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: the ability of the substance to cause delayed or latent effects over the lifetime of an organism, including carcinogenic, mutagenic or neurotoxic effects,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: the ability of the substance to cause survival impairment of an organism,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi.1)
Subparagraph.Text: the ability of the substance to disrupt the reproductive system or endocrine system of an organism,

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 68(a)(xii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (xii)
Subparagraph.Text: the existence, development and use of safer or more sustainable alternatives to the substance or product,

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 68(a) of the Act is amended by striking out âandâ at the end of subparagraph (xiii), by adding âandâ at the end of subparagraph (xiv) and by adding the following after subparagraph (xiv):

Subparagraph.Label: (xv)
Subparagraph.Text: the manner in which the public may be provided with information regarding the substance or product, including, in the case of a product, by labelling it;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 68(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: provide information and make recommendations respecting any matter in relation to the substance or product, including measures to control the presence of the substance or product in the environment.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 69(2.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Nothing in subsection (2) shall prevent either Minister or both Ministers from exercising the powers under subsection (1) at any time after the 60th day following the day on which an offer is made under subsection (2).
Nothing in subsection (2) shall prevent the Minister from exercising the powers under subsection (1) at any time after the sixtieth day following the day an offer is made under subsection (2).

Guidelines issued under this section shall be made available to the public, and the Minister who issued the guidelines shall give notice of them in the Canada Gazette and in any other manner that the Minister considers appropriate.
Subsection 69(3) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Guidelines issued under this section shall be made available to the public, and the Minister who issued the guidelines shall give notice of them in the Canada Gazette and in any other manner that that Minister considers appropriate.
The portion of subsection 71(1) of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Notice requiring information, samples or testing

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, for the purpose of assessing whether a substance is toxic or is capable of becoming toxic â or for the purpose of assessing whether to control, or the manner in which to control, a substance, a product that contains a substance or a product that may release a substance into the environment â including a substance specified on the list of toxic substances in Schedule 1,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: publish in the Canada Gazette and in any other manner that the Minister considers appropriate a notice requiring any person who is described in the notice and who is or was within the period specified in the notice engaged in any activity involving the substance or product, as the case may be, to notify the Minister that the person is or was during that period engaged in that activity;

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, for the purpose of assessing whether a substance is toxic or is capable of becoming toxic, or for the purpose of assessing whether to control, or the manner in which to control, a substance, including a substance specified on the List of Toxic Substances in Schedule 1,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: publish in the Canada Gazette and in any other manner that the Minister considers appropriate a notice requiring any person who is described in the notice and who is or was within the period specified in the notice engaged in any activity involving the substance to notify the Minister that the person is or was during that period engaged in that activity;
Â

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: subject to section 72, send a written notice to any person who is described in the notice and who is or was within the period specified in the notice engaged in any activity involving the importation or manufacturing of the substance or any product containing the substance requiring the person to conduct toxicological and other tests that the Minister may specify in the notice and submit the results of the tests to the Minister.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 71(1)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: subject to section 72, send a written notice to any person who is described in the notice and who is or was within the period specified in the notice engaged in any activity involving the importation or manufacturing of the substance or product, as the case may be, requiring the person to conduct toxicological or other tests that the Minister may specify in the notice and submit the results of the tests to the Minister.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 71(2) of the English version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
A notice published under paragraph (1)(b) may require any information and samples, including
A notice sent under paragraph (1)(b) may require any information and samples, including

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of a substance, available toxicological information, available monitoring information, samples of the substance and information on the quantities, composition, uses and distribution of the substance and products containing the substance; and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 71(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of a substance, available toxicological information, available monitoring information, samples of the substance and information on the quantities, composition, uses and distribution of the substance;

Text.Ins: in respect of a product that contains a substance or that may release a substance into the environment, information on the quantities, composition, manufacturing, processing, packaging, labelling, uses and distribution of the product;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 71(2) of the Act is amended by adding âandâ at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: information on the method used to quantify any information that is provided.

Subsection.Text: Section 71 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Method and manner â notice under paragraph (1)(b)
Subsection.Text: A notice published under paragraph (1)(b) may specify the method to be used to quantify any information that is to be provided and the manner in which that information and any sample is to be provided.
A notice sent under paragraph (1)(c) may, among other things, require the person to provide any information and samples, including

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of a substance or of a product that contains a substance or that may release a substance into the environment, toxicological information, monitoring information, test samples and information on the quantities, composition, uses and distribution of the substance or product; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: information on the methods, test procedures and laboratory practices followed for performing any required test and on the conditions under which those tests are conducted.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Method and manner â notice under paragraph (1)(c)
Subsection.Text: The notice sent under paragraph (1)(c) may, among other things, specify

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the conditions, methods, test procedures and laboratory practices to be followed for conducting sampling, analyses, measurements, quantification or monitoring as part of any required test;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the manner in which the test results are to be submitted;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the method to be used to quantify any information that is to be provided; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the manner in which that information and any sample is to be provided.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 71(4) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Despite subsection (3), the Minister may, on request in writing from any person to whom a notice referred to in any of paragraphs (1)(a) to (c) is directed or sent, extend the time or times within which the person shall comply with the notice.

Despite subsection (3), the Minister may, on request in writing from any person to whom a notice referred to in paragraph (1)(a), (b) or (c) has been sent, extend the time or times within which the person shall comply with the notice.

Section.Text: Sections 72 to 74 of the Act are replaced by the following:

The Minister may not exercise the power under paragraph 71(1)(c) in relation to a substance, a product that contains a substance or a product that may release a substance into the environment unless the Ministers have reason to suspect that the substance is toxic or capable of becoming toxic or it has been determined under this Act that the substance is toxic or capable of becoming toxic.

Subsection.Text: The Ministers shall, within two years after the day on which this section receives royal assent, develop and publish a plan

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that specifies the substances to which the Ministers are satisfied priority should be given in assessing whether they are toxic or capable of becoming toxic; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that may specify the activities or initiatives in relation to assessing, controlling or otherwise managing the risks to the environment or to human health posed by substances that are or will be undertaken under an Act of Parliament for whose administration either Minister is responsible and which the Ministers are of the opinion should be prioritized.

Subsection.Text: The plan shall also include the period after which the Ministers will review the plan.
In developing a proposed plan, and in implementing the plan, the Ministers

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: may consult with the Committee, a government department or agency, aboriginal people, representatives of industry and labour and municipal authorities or with persons interested in the quality of the environment or the preservation and improvement of public health;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: shall consider whether assessing substances by class is more advantageous than assessing them individually; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: shall take into account the matters referred to in paragraph 68(a).

Subsection.Text: The Minister shall publish the proposed plan in the Environmental Registry and shall give notice in the Canada Gazette and in any other manner that the Minister considers appropriate of the proposed planâs availability.
Within 60 days after the publication of the notice, any person may file with the Minister written comments on the proposed plan.
The Ministers shall take into account the comments and may amend the proposed plan in any manner they consider appropriate based on those comments.
The Minister shall publish the plan in the Canada Gazette and in any other manner that the Minister considers appropriate.
If after reviewing the plan the Ministers propose to amend it, subsections (2) to (7) apply to the proposed amended plan.

Section.Text: The Ministers shall include in the annual report required by section 342 a report on the progress made in assessing the substances specified in the plan developed under section 73 and in respect of any activities or initiatives specified in the plan.

The Minister may not exercise the power under paragraph 71(1)(c) in relation to a substance unless the Ministers have reason to suspect that the substance is toxic or capable of becoming toxic or it has been determined under this Act that the substance is toxic or capable of becoming toxic.

Subsection.Text: The Ministers shall, within seven years from the giving of Royal Assent to this Act, categorize the substances that are on the Domestic Substances List by virtue of section 66, for the purpose of identifying the substances on the List that, in their opinion and on the basis of available information,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: may present, to individuals in Canada, the greatest potential for exposure; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: are persistent or bioaccumulative in accordance with the regulations, and inherently toxic to human beings or to non-human organisms, as determined by laboratory or other studies.

Subsection.Text: Where available information is insufficient to identify substances as referred to in that subsection, the Ministers may, to the extent possible, cooperate with other governments in Canada, governments of foreign states or any interested persons to acquire the information required for the identification.

When categorizing substances under subsection (1), the Ministers shall examine the substances that are on the Domestic Substances List to determine whether an amendment should be made to the List to indicate that subsection 81(3) applies with respect to those substances.

Section.Text: The Ministers shall conduct a screening assessment of a substance in order to determine whether the substance is toxic or capable of becoming toxic and shall propose one of the measures described in subsection 77(2) if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Ministers identify a substance on the Domestic Substances List to be a substance described in paragraph 73(1)(a) or (b); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the substance has been added to the Domestic Substances List under section 105.

Section.Text: Sections 76 and 76.1 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: List of substances capable of becoming toxic

Subsection.Text: The Minister shall compile and may amend from time to time a list that specifies substances that the Ministers have reason to suspect are capable of becoming toxic or that have been determined to be capable of becoming toxic.
The List may include information regarding a substance specified on the List, including an indication that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the substance is specified on the Domestic Substances List with an indication that subsection 81(3) or 106(3) applies with respect to the substance; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister has published a notice in the Canada Gazette indicating that subsection 81(4) or 106(4) applies with respect to the substance.

Subsection.Text: The Minister shall publish the List and any amendments to it in the Environmental Registry and in any other manner that the Minister considers appropriate.
The List is not a statutory instrument as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Statutory Instruments Act.
Any person may file in writing with the Minister a request that the Ministers assess a substance to determine whether it is toxic or capable of becoming toxic.

The Ministers shall consider the request and, within 90 days after the day on which the request is filed, the Minister shall inform the person who filed the request of how the Ministers intend to deal with it and the reasons for dealing with it in that manner.
A request shall be filed in the form and manner and shall contain the information specified by the Minister.

Section.MarginalNote: Weight of evidence and precautionary principle

Subsection.Text: The Ministers shall apply a weight of evidence approach and the precautionary principle when they are conducting and interpreting the results of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an assessment conducted under this Part, other than under section 83, in order to determine whether a substance is toxic or capable of becoming toxic; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a review of a decision of another jurisdiction under subsection 75(3) that, in their opinion, is based on scientific considerations and is relevant to Canada.

Subsection.Text: When the Ministers are conducting and interpreting the results of an assessment or review referred to in subsection (1), they shall consider available information on any vulnerable population in relation to the substance and on the cumulative effects that may result from exposure to the substance in combination with exposure to other substances.

The Ministers shall compile and may amend from time to time in accordance with subsection (5) a list, to be known as the Priority Substances List, and the List shall specify substances in respect of which the Ministers are satisfied priority should be given in assessing whether they are toxic or capable of becoming toxic.

For the purposes of subsection (1), the Minister shall offer to consult with the government of a province and the members of the Committee who are representatives of aboriginal governments and may consult with a government department or agency, aboriginal people, representatives of industry and labour and municipal authorities or with persons interested in the quality of the environment or the preservation and improvement of public health.

At any time after the 60th day following the day on which the Minister offers to consult in accordance with subsection (2), the Minister may act under subsection (1) if the offer to consult is not accepted by the government of a province or members of the Committee who are representatives of aboriginal governments.
Any person may file in writing with the Minister a request that a substance be added to the Priority Substances List and the request shall state the reasons for adding the substance to the List.

The Ministers shall consider a request filed under subsection (3) and, within 90 days after the request is filed, the Minister shall inform the person who filed the request of how the Minister intends to deal with it and the reasons for dealing with it in that manner.
The Ministers may amend the Priority Substances List

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: by adding a substance to the List where the Ministers are satisfied on the basis of a determination made as a result of a screening assessment conducted under section 74, the review of a decision of another jurisdiction under subsection 75(3), consultation under subsection (2) or a request made under subsection (3) or for any other reason that priority should be given in assessing whether the substance is toxic or capable of becoming toxic; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: by deleting a substance from the List where the Ministers have determined whether the substance is toxic or capable of becoming toxic.

Subsection.Text: The Minister shall publish in the Canada Gazette and in any other manner that the Minister considers appropriate the Priority Substances List and any amendments to the List.

Section.Text: When the Ministers are conducting and interpreting the results of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a screening assessment under section 74,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a review of a decision of another jurisdiction under subsection 75(3) that, in their opinion, is based on scientific considerations and is relevant to Canada, or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an assessment whether a substance specified on the Priority Substances List is toxic or capable of becoming toxic,

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: the Ministers shall apply a weight of evidence approach and the precautionary principle.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 77(1) to (4) of the Act are replaced by the following:

If the Ministers have conducted an assessment under this Part, other than under section 83, to determine whether a substance is toxic or capable of becoming toxic or a review of a decision of another jurisdiction under subsection 75(3) that, in their opinion, is based on scientific considerations and is relevant to Canada, the Ministers shall publish in the Canada Gazette, and either Minister may publish in any other manner that that Minister considers appropriate,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a statement indicating one of the measures referred to in subsection (2) that the Ministers propose to take and a summary of the scientific considerations on the basis of which the measure is proposed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the measure is one referred to in paragraph (2)(c) or (d) and the Ministers are of the opinion that the substance is regulated by any other Act of Parliament, or a regulation or instrument made under that Act, in a manner that provides sufficient protection to the environment and human health, a statement made jointly by the Minister and the Minister responsible for the administration of that Act identifying the Act, regulation or instrument and indicating the manner in which the substance is regulated by it.

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (3), for the purposes of subsection (1), the Ministers shall propose one of the following measures:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: taking no further action in respect of the substance;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: unless the substance is already on the List referred to in section 75.1, adding the substance to that List;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: recommending that the substance be added to Part 1 of the list of toxic substances in Schedule 1; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: recommending that the substance be added to Part 2 of the list of toxic substances in Schedule 1.

Subsection.Text: The Ministers shall propose to take the measure referred to in paragraph (2)(c) if the substance is determined to be toxic and the Ministers are satisfied that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the substance may have a long-term harmful effect on the environment and

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is persistent and bioaccumulative in accordance with the regulations,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: is present in the environment primarily as a result of human activity, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: is not a naturally occurring radionuclide or a naturally occurring inorganic substance; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the substance

Subparagraph.Text: is inherently toxic to human beings or non-human organisms, as determined by laboratory or other studies, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: is, in accordance with the regulations, a substance that poses the highest risk.

Subsection.Text: Where the Ministers have conducted

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a screening assessment under section 74,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a review of a decision of another jurisdiction under subsection 75(3) that, in their opinion, is based on scientific considerations and is relevant to Canada, or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an assessment whether a substance specified on the Priority Substances List is toxic or capable of becoming toxic,

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: the Ministers shall publish in the Canada Gazette and either Minister may publish in any other manner that that Minister considers appropriate a statement indicating one of the measures referred to in subsection (2) that the Ministers propose to take and a summary of the scientific considerations on the basis of which the measure is proposed.

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (3), for the purposes of subsection (1), the Ministers shall propose one of the following measures:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: taking no further action in respect of the substance;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: unless the substance is already on the Priority Substances List, adding the substance to the Priority Substances List; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: recommending that the substance be added to the List of Toxic Substances in Schedule 1 and, where applicable under subsection (4), the implementation of virtual elimination under subsection 65(3).

Subsection.Text: Where, based on a screening assessment conducted under section 74, the substance is determined to be toxic or capable of becoming toxic, and the Ministers are satisfied that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the substance may have a long-term harmful effect on the environment and is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: persistent and bioaccumulative in accordance with the regulations, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: inherently toxic to human beings or non-human organisms, as determined by laboratory or other studies, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the presence of the substance in the environment results primarily from human activity,

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: the Ministers shall propose to take the measure referred to in paragraph (2)(c).

Subsection.Text: Where the Ministers propose to take the measure referred to in paragraph (2)(c) in respect of a substance and the Ministers are satisfied that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the substance is persistent and bioaccumulative in accordance with the regulations,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the presence of the substance in the environment results primarily from human activity, and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the substance is not a naturally occurring radionuclide or a naturally occurring inorganic substance,

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: the Ministers shall propose the implementation of virtual elimination under subsection 65(3) of the substance.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 77(6) to (8) of the Act are replaced by the following:
After taking into consideration in an expeditious manner the comments filed under subsection (5), the Ministers shall publish in the Canada Gazette

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a summary of the assessment or of the review, as the case may be, referred to in subsection (1);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a statement indicating the measure that the Ministers propose to take; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the measure is one referred to in paragraph (2)(c) or (d),

Subparagraph.Text: a statement indicating the manner in which a proposed regulation or instrument respecting preventive or control actions in relation to the substance is to be developed under this or any other Act of Parliament, or

Text.Ins: if the Ministers are of the opinion that the substance is regulated by any other Act of Parliament, or a regulation or instrument made under that Act, in a manner that provides sufficient protection to the environment and human health, a statement made jointly by the Minister and the Minister responsible for the administration of that Act identifying the Act, regulation or instrument and indicating the manner in which the substance is regulated by it.

Subsection.Text: If the Ministers publish a statement under paragraph (6)(b) in respect of a substance already specified on the list of toxic substances in Schedule 1, paragraph (6)(c) does not apply.
After taking into consideration in an expeditious manner the comments filed under subsection (5), the Ministers shall publish in the Canada Gazette

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a summary of the screening assessment conducted under section 74, of the review of a decision of another jurisdiction under subsection 75(3) or of a report of the assessment of substances specified on the Priority Substances List, as the case may be;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a statement indicating the measure that the Ministers propose to take; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: where the measure is that referred to in paragraph (2)(c), a statement indicating the manner in which the Ministers intend to develop a proposed regulation or instrument respecting preventive or control actions in relation to the substance.

Subsection.Text: Where the Ministers publish a statement under subsection (6) in respect of a substance specified on the Priority Substances List, the Ministers shall make a report of the assessment of the substance available to the public.

Where the Ministers make an assessment whether a substance specified on the Priority Substances List is toxic or is capable of becoming toxic and decide not to recommend that the substance be added to the List of Toxic Substances in Schedule 1, any person may, within 60 days after publication of the decision in the Canada Gazette, file a notice of objection with the Minister requesting that a board of review be established under section 333 and stating the reason for the objection.
Subsection 77(9) of the Act is replaced by the following:

When the Ministers publish a statement under paragraph (6)(b) indicating that the measure that they propose to take is a recommendation that the substance be added to Part 1 or 2 of the list of toxic substances in Schedule 1, the Ministers shall make a recommendation for an order under subsection 90(1), and, if the substance is already specified in the other Part, the Ministers shall make a recommendation for an order under subsection 90(2).

The Ministers shall make a recommendation for an order under subsection 90(1) when publishing a statement under paragraph (6)(b) indicating that the measure that they propose to take is a recommendation that the substance be added to the List of Toxic Substances in Schedule 1.

Section.Text: Sections 78 and 79 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If the Ministers publish a statement under subparagraph 77(6)(c)(i) indicating that more than one proposed regulation or instrument respecting preventive or control actions in relation to a substance is to be developed, the Minister shall, when the first regulation or instrument respecting preventive or control actions in relation to the substance is published under paragraph 92(1)(a) or a statement identifying the first such regulation or instrument is published under paragraph 92(1)(b) or (c), as the case may be, publish in the Environmental Registry and in any other manner that the Minister considers appropriate a statement respecting the development of the subsequent proposed regulations or instruments that specifies, to the extent possible, an estimated time frame within which those proposed regulations or instruments are to be developed.

If the Ministers amend the statement respecting the development of the subsequent proposed regulations or instruments, the Minister shall publish that statement as amended in the Environmental Registry and in any other manner that the Minister considers appropriate.

Subject to subsections (2) to (4), where a substance has been specified on the Priority Substances List for a period of five years and the Ministers have not yet determined whether the substance is toxic or capable of becoming toxic, any person may file a notice of objection with the Minister requesting that a board of review be established under section 333.

Where a substance is specified on the Priority Substances List and the Ministers are satisfied that new or additional information is required to assess whether the substance is toxic or capable of becoming toxic, the Minister shall publish a notice in the Canada Gazette indicating

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that the period of five years referred to in subsection (1) is suspended and the duration of the suspension; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the new or additional information that is required to assess whether the substance is toxic or capable of becoming toxic, unless another provision of this Part requires the submission of the new or additional information.

Subsection.Text: Where a notice is published under subsection (2), the operation of subsection (1) in relation to the substance is suspended until the earlier of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the expiry of the period determined by the Ministers, notice of which is given in the Canada Gazette, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the time when the required information becomes available to the Ministers.

Subsection.Text: Where a notice is published under subsection (2) and the Ministers have not yet determined whether the substance is toxic or capable of becoming toxic within a period of two years after the date on which the suspension referred to in the notice ends, any person may file a notice of objection with the Minister requesting that a board of review be established under section 333.

Where the Minister publishes in the Canada Gazette under subsection 77(6) a statement indicating that the proposed measure, as confirmed or amended, is the implementation of virtual elimination under subsection 65(3) in respect of a substance, the Minister shall in that statement require any person who is described in it to prepare and submit to the Minister a plan in respect of the substance in relation to the work, undertaking or activity of the person.
Every person who is required to prepare and submit a plan under subsection (1)

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: shall include in it a description of the proposed actions in respect of the implementation of virtual elimination under subsection 65(3) of the substance in relation to the work, undertaking or activity of the person and the period within which the proposed actions are to be completed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: may include in it relevant information respecting measurable quantities or concentrations of the substance, environmental or health risks and social, economic or technical matters.

Subsection.Text: Every person to whom a statement referred to in subsection (1) is directed shall comply with it within the period specified in the statement.

The period of time to be specified in the statement shall begin no earlier than the date on which an order is made under subsection 90(1) adding the substance to the List of Toxic Substances in Schedule 1.

Section.Text: Subsection 84(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Any prohibition on the manufacture or importation of a substance imposed under paragraph (1)(b) expires two years after the day on which it is imposed unless, before the expiry of the two years, there is published in the Canada Gazette

Paragraph.Text: a regulation respecting preventive or control actions, in relation to the substance, that is proposed to be made under this Act; or

Text.Ins: a statement, identifying a regulation respecting preventive or control actions in relation to the substance that is proposed to be made under any other Act of Parliament,

made jointly by the Minister and the Minister responsible for the administration of the Act of Parliament under which the regulation is to be made, if the Act is not an Act referred to in subparagraph (ii), or
made by the Minister of Health, if the Act of Parliament is one for whose administration that Minister is responsible.

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: If such a proposed regulation â or such a statement identifying a proposed regulation â is so published, the prohibition expires on the day on which the regulation comes into force.

Subsection.Text: Any prohibition on the manufacture or importation of a substance imposed under paragraph (1)(b) expires two years after it is imposed unless, before the expiry of the two years, the Governor in Council publishes in the Canada Gazette a notice of proposed regulations under section 93 in respect of the substance, in which case the prohibition expires on the day the regulations come into force.

Section.Text: Subsections 85(2) and (3) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A notice referred to in subsection (1)

Paragraph.Text: shall indicate, by inclusion or exclusion, the significant new activities in relation to the substance with respect to which subsection 81(4) is to apply and, if regulations in respect of those significant new activities are not made under paragraphs 89(1)(c), (d) and (g), specify the information to be provided to the Minister under that subsection, the date on or before which it is to be provided and the period within which it is to be assessed under section 83; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: may specify for the purpose of subsection 86(2) classes of persons who are not required to be notified under subsection 86(1) with respect to the substance.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, by notice published in the Canada Gazette, specify for the purpose of subsection 86(2) classes of persons who are not required to be notified under subsection 86(1) with respect to a substance for which a notice was published under subsection (1), if no such class was specified in that notice.
The Minister may, by notice published in the Canada Gazette,

Paragraph.Text: vary the significant new activities in relation to a substance for which a notice has been published under subsection (1) or vary the information to be provided to the Minister under subsection 81(4), the date on or before which that information is to be provided or the period within which it is to be assessed under section 83;
indicate that subsection 81(4) no longer applies with respect to the substance;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: vary the classes of persons, if any, that are specified for the purpose of subsection 86(2) in a notice published under subsection (1) or (3); or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: indicate that a class of persons is no longer specified for the purpose of subsection 86(2).

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, by notice published in the Canada Gazette, vary the significant new activities in relation to a substance for which a notice has been given under subsection (1) or indicate that subsection 81(4) no longer applies with respect to that substance.

A notice referred to in subsection (1) or (2) shall indicate, by inclusion or exclusion, the significant new activities in relation to the substance in respect of which subsection 81(4) is to apply, and if regulations in respect of those significant new activities are not made under paragraphs 89(1)(c), (d) and (g), specify the information to be provided to the Minister under that subsection, the date within which it is to be provided and the period within which it is to be assessed under section 83.

Section.Text: Section 86 of the Act is renumbered as subsection 86(1) and is amended by adding the following:

Subsection.Text: A person who is within a class of persons that is specified in a notice published under subsection 85(1) or (3) â or, if the class is varied by a notice published under subsection 85(4), within the class as varied â is not required to be notified under subsection (1) with respect to the substance to which the notice relates.

Section.Text: Subsections 87(3) and (4) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If a substance is on the Domestic Substances List or is to be added to the List under subsection (1) or 66.1(1), the Minister may amend the List to indicate that subsection 81(3) applies with respect to the substance.
An amendment referred to in subsection (3)

Paragraph.Text: shall indicate, by inclusion or exclusion, the significant new activities in relation to the substance with respect to which subsection 81(3) is to apply and, if regulations in respect of those significant new activities are not made under paragraphs 89(1)(c), (d) and (g), specify the information to be provided to the Minister under that subsection, the date on or before which it is to be provided and the period within which it is to be assessed under section 83; and

Text.Ins: may specify for the purpose of subsection 87.1(2) classes of persons who are not required to be notified under subsection 87.1(1) with respect to the substance.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may amend the Domestic Substances List in respect of a substance with respect to which subsection 81(3) applies

Paragraph.Text: to vary the significant new activities in relation to the substance or vary the information to be provided to the Minister under subsection 81(3), the date on or before which that information is to be provided or the period within which it is to be assessed under section 83;
to indicate that subsection 81(3) no longer applies;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: to specify classes of persons for the purpose of subsection 87.1(2), if no such classes are specified with respect to the substance;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: to vary the classes of persons, if any, that are specified for the purpose of subsection 87.1(2); or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: to delete any class of persons that is specified for the purpose of subsection 87.1(2).

Subsection.Text: Where a substance is on the Domestic Substances List or is to be added to the List under subsection (1), the Minister may amend the List in respect of the substance to indicate that subsection 81(3) applies with respect to the substance or that it no longer applies or by varying the significant new activities in relation to the substance in respect of which subsection 81(3) is to apply.

An amendment referred to in subsection (3) shall indicate, by inclusion or exclusion, the significant new activities in relation to the substance in respect of which subsection 81(3) is to apply, and if regulations in respect of those significant new activities are not made under paragraphs 89(1)(c), (d) and (g), specify the information to be provided to the Minister under that subsection, the date within which it is to be provided and the period within which it is to be assessed under section 83.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 87:

Section.MarginalNote: Notification of persons required to comply

Subsection.Text: If a substance is specified on the Domestic Substances List with an indication that subsection 81(3) applies with respect to the substance, every person who transfers the physical possession or control of the substance shall notify all persons to whom the possession or control is transferred of the obligation to comply with that subsection.

A person is not required to be notified under subsection (1) with respect to a substance if they are within a class of persons that is specified on the Domestic Substances List in respect of the substance for the purpose of this subsection.

Section.Text: Paragraph 89(1)(k) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: generally for carrying out the purposes and provisions of sections 66 to 66.2 and 80 to 88.

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Ministers, make regulations

Paragraph.Text: Â

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: generally for carrying out the purposes and provisions of sections 66 and 80 to 88.

Section.Text: Subsections 90(1) to (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Addition to list of toxic substances

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (3), the Governor in Council may, if satisfied that a substance is toxic, on the recommendation of the Ministers, make an order adding the substance to Part 1 or 2 of the list of toxic substances in Schedule 1.

In developing a proposed regulation or instrument respecting preventive or control actions in relation to a substance specified on the list of toxic substances in Schedule 1, the Ministers shall give priority to,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a substance specified in Part 1 of the list of toxic substances in that Schedule, the total, partial or conditional prohibition of activities in relation to the substance or of releases of the substance into the environment; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a substance specified in Part 2 of the list of toxic substances in that Schedule, pollution prevention actions.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of paragraph (1.1)(a), the Ministers may consider any factor that they consider appropriate, including whether the activity or release can be undertaken in a manner that minimizes or eliminates any harmful effect on the environment or human health and whether there are feasible alternatives to the substance.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Deletion from the list of toxic substances
Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (3), the Governor in Council may, if satisfied that the inclusion of a substance specified in Part 1 or 2 of the list of toxic substances in Schedule 1 is no longer necessary, on the recommendation of the Ministers, make an order deleting the substance from that Part and repealing the regulations made under section 93 with respect to the substance.

Subject to subsection (3), the Governor in Council may, if satisfied that a substance is toxic, on the recommendation of the Ministers, make an order adding the substance to the List of Toxic Substances in Schedule 1.

In developing proposed regulations or instruments respecting preventive or control actions in relation to substances specified on the List of Toxic Substances in Schedule 1, the Ministers shall give priority to pollution prevention actions.

Subject to subsection (3), the Governor in Council may, if satisfied that the inclusion of a substance specified on the List of Toxic Substances in Schedule 1 is no longer necessary, on the recommendation of the Ministers, make an order

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: deleting the substance from the List and deleting the type of regulations specified in the List as being applicable with respect to the substance; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: repealing the regulations made under section 93 with respect to the substance.

Section.Text: Subsections 91(1) to (5) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Publication of proposed preventive or control actions

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (2), (6) and (7), within two years after the publication of the Ministersâ statement under paragraph 77(6)(b) indicating that the measure that they propose to take, as confirmed or amended, in respect of a substance is a recommendation that the substance be added to Part 1 or 2 of the list of toxic substances in Schedule 1,

Paragraph.Text: the Minister shall publish in the Canada Gazette

Subparagraph.Text: a regulation or instrument respecting preventive or control actions, in relation to the substance, that is proposed to be made under this Act, other than an instrument that may be made only by the Minister of Health, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a statement â identifying a regulation or instrument respecting preventive or control actions, in relation to the substance, that is proposed to be made under any other Act of Parliament other than an Act for whose administration the Minister of Health is responsible â made jointly by the Minister and the Minister responsible for the administration of the Act under which the regulation or instrument is to be made; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister of Health shall publish in the Canada Gazette

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an instrument respecting preventive or control actions, in relation to the substance, that is proposed to be made by that Minister under this Act, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a statement identifying a regulation or instrument respecting preventive or control actions in relation to the substance that is proposed to be made under any other Act of Parliament for whose administration that Minister is responsible.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply in respect of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a substance specified in Part 1 or 2 of the list of toxic substances in Schedule 1 in relation to which the measure the Ministers propose to take, as confirmed or amended, is a recommendation that the substance be added to the other Part; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a substance in relation to which a statement has been published under subparagraph 77(6)(c)(ii).

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (6) and (7), a proposed regulation or instrument respecting preventive or control actions in relation to a substance shall be published by the Minister in the Canada Gazette within two years after the publication of the Ministersâ statement under paragraph 77(6)(b) indicating that the measure that they propose to take, as confirmed or amended, is a recommendation that the substance be added to the List of Toxic Substances in Schedule 1.

A proposed regulation or instrument in respect of preventive or control actions in relation to a substance for which a statement has been published under subsection 77(6) indicating that the measure proposed by the Ministers is the implementation of virtual elimination under subsection 65(3) shall specify the dates on which the preventive or control actions are to take effect.

In establishing the quantity or concentration that is measurable in relation to a substance for the purposes of a proposed regulation or instrument referred to in subsection (2), the Ministers shall take into consideration information concerning sensitive and readily available analytical methods and any relevant information contained in plans referred to in subsection 79(2).

The Minister shall, where applicable, publish in the Canada Gazette a statement accompanying the proposed regulation or instrument for a substance referred to in subsection (2) describing any additional measures that the Ministers intend to recommend with respect to the implementation of virtual elimination under subsection 65(3) and summarizing their reasons for so intending.

In determining the preventive or control actions in relation to a substance and the dates on which those actions are to take effect that are to be set out in a proposed regulation or instrument referred to in subsection (2), and in determining any additional measures described in a statement published under subsection (4), the Ministers shall take into consideration any factor or information that, in the opinion of the Ministers, is relevant, including

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: information contained in plans referred to in section 79; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: environmental or health risks identified in the summary published under subsection 77(6) and any other relevant social, economic or technical matters.

Section.Text: Subsection 92(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Publication of preventive or control actions

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2), within 18 months after the day on which a proposed regulation or instrument is published under subparagraph 91(1)(a)(i) or (b)(i) or subsection 91(6) or a statement identifying a proposed regulation or instrument is published under subparagraph 91(1)(a)(ii) or (b)(ii), unless a material substantive change is required to be made to that proposed regulation or instrument,

Paragraph.Text: a regulation or instrument respecting preventive or control actions in relation to a substance shall be made under this Act and published in the Canada Gazette;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister shall publish in the Canada Gazette a statement â identifying a regulation or instrument respecting preventive or control actions in relation to a substance that was made under any other Act of Parliament â made jointly by the Minister and the Minister responsible for the administration of the Act under which the regulation or instrument is made; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister of Health shall publish in the Canada Gazette a statement identifying a regulation or instrument respecting preventive or control actions in relation to a substance that was made under any other Act of Parliament for whose administration that Minister is responsible.
