Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2), any regulation or instrument respecting preventive or control actions in relation to a substance shall be made and published in the Canada Gazette within 18 months after the publication of the proposed regulation or instrument under subsection 91(1) or (6), unless a material substantive change is required to be made to it.

Section.Text: Section 92.1 of the Act is repealed.

For the purposes of subsection 65(3), the Ministers may make regulations prescribing the quantity or concentration of a substance that may be released into the environment either alone or in combination with any other substance from any source or type of source.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 93(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subject to subsections (3) and (4), the Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Ministers, make regulations with respect to a substance specified on the list of toxic substances in Schedule 1, including regulations providing for, or imposing requirements respecting,

Subject to subsections (3) and (4), the Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Ministers, make regulations with respect to a substance specified on the List of Toxic Substances in Schedule 1, including regulations providing for, or imposing requirements respecting,

Paragraph.Text: Â

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the purposes for which the substance or a product containing it may be imported, manufactured, processed, used, offered for sale or sold;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the manner in which and conditions under which the substance or a product containing it may be imported, manufactured, processed or used;
Â

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: the quantities or concentrations of the substance that may be imported;
Â

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: the total, partial or conditional prohibition of the manufacture, use, processing, sale, offering for sale, import or export of the substance or a product containing it;

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: the total, partial or conditional prohibition of the import or export of a product that is intended to contain the substance;

Paragraph.Label: (n)
Paragraph.Text: the quantity or concentration of the substance that may be contained in any product manufactured, imported, exported, offered for sale or sold in Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (o)
Paragraph.Text: the manner in which, conditions under which and the purposes for which the substance or a product containing it may be advertised or offered for sale;

Paragraph.Label: (p)
Paragraph.Text: the manner in which and conditions under which the substance or a product containing it may be stored, displayed, handled, transported or offered for transport;

Paragraph.Label: (q)
Paragraph.Text: the packaging and labelling of the substance or a product containing it;

Paragraph.Label: (r)
Paragraph.Text: the manner, conditions, places and method of disposal of the substance or a product containing it, including standards for the construction, maintenance and inspection of disposal sites;

Paragraph.Label: (s)
Paragraph.Text: the submission to the Minister, on request or at any prescribed times, of information relating to the substance;
Â

Paragraph.Label: (u)
Paragraph.Text: the conduct of sampling, analyses, tests, measurements or monitoring of the substance and the submission of the results to the Minister;

Paragraph.Label: (v)
Paragraph.Text: the submission of samples of the substance to the Minister;

Paragraph.Label: (w)
Paragraph.Text: the conditions, test procedures and laboratory practices to be followed for conducting sampling, analyses, tests, measurements or monitoring of the substance;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 93(1)(f) and (g) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the purposes for which the substance, a product that contains the substance or a product that may release the substance into the environment may be imported, exported, manufactured, processed, used, offered for sale or sold;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the manner in which and conditions under which the substance, a product that contains the substance or a product that may release the substance into the environment may be imported, exported, manufactured, processed or used;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 93(1)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: the quantities or concentrations of the substance that may be imported or exported;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 93(1)(l) to (s) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: the total, partial or conditional prohibition of the manufacture, use, processing, sale, offering for sale, import or export of the substance, a product that contains the substance or a product that may release the substance into the environment;

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: the total, partial or conditional prohibition of the manufacture, import or export of a product that is intended to contain the substance;

Paragraph.Label: (n)
Paragraph.Text: the quantity or concentration of the substance that may be contained in any product manufactured, imported, exported, offered for sale or sold in Canada, or that any such product may release into the environment;

Paragraph.Label: (o)
Paragraph.Text: the manner in which, conditions under which and the purposes for which the substance, a product that contains the substance or a product that may release the substance into the environment may be advertised or offered for sale;

Paragraph.Label: (p)
Paragraph.Text: the manner in which and conditions under which the substance, a product that contains the substance or a product that may release the substance into the environment may be stored, displayed, handled, transported or offered for transport;

Paragraph.Label: (q)
Paragraph.Text: the packaging and labelling of the substance, a product that contains the substance or a product that may release the substance into the environment;

Paragraph.Label: (r)
Paragraph.Text: the manner, conditions, places and method of disposal of the substance, a product that contains the substance or a product that may release the substance into the environment, including standards for the construction, maintenance and inspection of disposal sites;

Paragraph.Label: (s)
Paragraph.Text: the submission to the Minister, on request or at any prescribed times, of information relating to the substance, a product that contains the substance or a product that may release the substance into the environment;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 93(1)(u) and (v) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (u)
Paragraph.Text: the conduct of sampling, analyses, tests, measurements or monitoring of the substance, a product that contains the substance or a product that may release the substance into the environment and the submission of the results to the Minister;

Paragraph.Label: (v)
Paragraph.Text: the submission of samples to the Minister of the substance, a product that contains the substance or a product that may release the substance into the environment;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 93(1)(w) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (w)
Paragraph.Text: the conditions, test procedures and laboratory practices to be followed for conducting sampling, analyses, tests, measurements or monitoring of the substance, a product that contains the substance or a product that may release the substance into the environment;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 93(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Regulations made under this section may authorize the Minister, in the circumstances and subject to the conditions and limits that may be specified in the regulations, to issue, amend, suspend and revoke permits and other authorizations in relation to any matter that is the subject of the regulations and to set their terms and conditions.
A permit or other authorization issued under regulations made under this section is not a statutory instrument as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Statutory Instruments Act.

A regulation made under subsection (1) with respect to a substance may amend the List of Toxic Substances in Schedule 1 so as to specify the type of regulation that applies with respect to the substance.
Subsection 94(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Minister may make an interim order in respect of a substance and the order may contain any provision that may be contained in a regulation made under subsection 93(1) if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the substance

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is not specified on the list of toxic substances in Schedule 1 and the Ministers believe that it is toxic or capable of becoming toxic, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: is specified on that list and the Ministers believe that it is not adequately regulated; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Ministers believe that immediate action is required to deal with a significant danger to the environment or to human life or health.

Subsection.Text: Where

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a substance

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is not specified on the List of Toxic Substances in Schedule 1 and the Ministers believe that it is toxic or capable of becoming toxic, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: is specified on that List and the Ministers believe that it is not adequately regulated, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Ministers believe that immediate action is required to deal with a significant danger to the environment or to human life or health,

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: the Minister may make an interim order in respect of the substance and the order may contain any provision that may be contained in a regulation made under subsection 93(1).

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 94(5)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the order was made in respect of a substance that was not specified on the list of toxic substances in Schedule 1, that the substance be added to Part 1 or 2 of that list under section 90.

Subsection.Text: Where the Governor in Council approves an interim order, the Ministers shall, within 90 days after the approval, publish in the Canada Gazette a statement indicating whether the Ministers intend to recommend to the Governor in Council

Paragraph.Text: Â

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the order was made in respect of a substance that was not specified on the List of Toxic Substances in Schedule 1, that the substance be added to that List under section 90.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 95(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

If there occurs or is a likelihood of a release into the environment of a substance specified on the list of toxic substances in Schedule 1 in contravention of a regulation made under section 93 or an order made under section 94, any person described in subsection (2) shall, as soon as possible in the circumstances,

Where there occurs or is a likelihood of a release into the environment of a substance specified on the List of Toxic Substances in Schedule 1 in contravention of a regulation made under section 92.1 or 93 or an order made under section 94, any person described in subsection (2) shall, as soon as possible in the circumstances,
Subsection 95(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If there occurs a release of a substance as described in subsection (1), any person, other than a person described in subsection (2), whose property is affected by the release and who knows that it is a substance specified on the list of toxic substances in Schedule 1 shall, as soon as possible in the circumstances and subject to subsection (4), report the matter to an enforcement officer or to any person that is designated by regulation.

Where there occurs a release of a substance as described in subsection (1), any person, other than a person described in subsection (2), whose property is affected by the release and who knows that it is a substance specified on the List of Toxic Substances in Schedule 1 shall, as soon as possible in the circumstances and subject to subsection (4), report the matter to an enforcement officer or to any person that is designated by regulation.

Section.Text: Subsection 96(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If a person has knowledge of the occurrence or likelihood of a release into the environment of a substance specified on the list of toxic substances in Schedule 1, but the person is not required to report the matter under this Act, the person may report any information relating to the release or likely release to an enforcement officer or to any person to whom a report may be made under section 95.

Where a person has knowledge of the occurrence or likelihood of a release into the environment of a substance specified on the List of Toxic Substances in Schedule 1, but the person is not required to report the matter under this Act, the person may report any information relating to the release or likely release to an enforcement officer or to any person to whom a report may be made under section 95.

Section.Text: The portion of section 99 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

If, in respect of a substance, a product that contains a substance or a product that may release a substance into the environment, there is a contravention of this Part or any regulation made under this Part, the Minister may, in writing,
Where, in respect of a substance or a product containing a substance, there is a contravention of this Part or any regulation made under this Part, the Minister may, in writing,
The portion of subsection 105(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Adding living organisms to Domestic Substances List

Subsection.Text: The Minister shall, for the purpose of section 106, add to the Domestic Substances List maintained under section 66 any living organism if the Minister is satisfied that, between January 1, 1984 and December 31, 1986, the living organism

The Minister shall, for the purposes of sections 74 and 106, add to the Domestic Substances List maintained under section 66 any living organism if the Minister is satisfied that, between January 1, 1984 and December 31, 1986, the living organism

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 105:

Section.MarginalNote: Domestic Substances List â Food and Drugs Act

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, for the purpose of section 106, add to the Domestic Substances List any living organism if the Minister is satisfied that, between January 1, 1987 and September 13, 2001, the living organism

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: was in Canadian commerce as a product to which the Food and Drugs Act applied; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: was in Canadian commerce as a living organism that was in or on such a product.

Subsection.Text: If the Minister includes a living organism on the Domestic Substances List and subsequently learns that, between January 1, 1987 and September 13, 2001, the requirements set out in paragraph (1)(a) or (b) were not met in respect of the living organism, the Minister shall delete the living organism from the List.
The Minister may, by order, designate any person or class of persons to exercise the powers and perform the duties and functions set out in this section.

If the Minister is satisfied that a living organism added to the Domestic Substances List under subsection 105(1) or 112(1) is not being manufactured in Canada or imported into Canada â or that a living organism added to the List under subsection 105.1(1) is not in Canadian commerce â the Minister may delete the living organism from the List.

Before deleting a living organism from the Domestic Substances List under subsection (1), the Minister shall publish in the Canada Gazette and in any other manner that the Minister considers appropriate a notice indicating the Ministerâs intention to delete it from the List.
Within 60 days after the publication of the notice, any person may file written comments with the Minister.
The Minister may, by order, designate any person or class of persons to exercise the powers and perform the duties and functions set out in this section.

Section.Text: Subsection 109(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Any prohibition on the manufacture or import of a living organism imposed under paragraph (1)(b) expires two years after the day on which it is imposed unless, before the expiry of the two years, there is published in the Canada Gazette

Paragraph.Text: a regulation respecting preventive or control actions, in relation to the living organism, that is proposed to be made under this Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a statement, identifying a regulation respecting preventive or control actions in relation to the living organism that is proposed to be made under any other Act of Parliament,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: made jointly by the Minister and the Minister responsible for the administration of the Act of Parliament under which the regulation is to be made, if the Act is not an Act referred to in subparagraph (ii), or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: made by the Minister of Health, if the Act of Parliament is one for whose administration that Minister is responsible.

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: If such a proposed regulation â or such a statement identifying a proposed regulation â is so published, the prohibition expires on the day on which the regulation comes into force.

Subsection.Text: Any prohibition on the manufacture or import of a living organism imposed under paragraph (1)(b) expires two years after it is imposed unless, before the expiry of the two years, the Governor in Council publishes in the Canada Gazette a notice of proposed regulations under section 114 in respect of the living organism, in which case the prohibition expires on the day the regulations come into force.

Section.Text: Subsections 110(2) and (3) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A notice referred to in subsection (1)

Paragraph.Text: shall indicate, by inclusion or exclusion, the significant new activities in relation to the living organism with respect to which subsection 106(4) is to apply and, if regulations in respect of those significant new activities are not made under paragraphs 114(1)(c), (d) and (g), specify the information to be provided to the Minister under that subsection, the date on or before which it is to be provided and the period within which it is to be assessed under section 108; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: may specify for the purpose of subsection 111(2) classes of persons who are not required to be notified under subsection 111(1) with respect to the living organism.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, by notice published in the Canada Gazette, specify for the purpose of subsection 111(2) classes of persons who are not required to be notified under subsection 111(1) with respect to a living organism for which a notice was published under subsection (1), if no such class was specified in that notice.
The Minister may, by notice published in the Canada Gazette,

Paragraph.Text: vary the significant new activities in relation to a living organism for which a notice has been published under subsection (1) or vary the information to be provided to the Minister under subsection 106(4), the date on or before which that information is to be provided or the period within which it is to be assessed under section 108;
indicate that subsection 106(4) no longer applies with respect to that living organism;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: vary the classes of persons, if any, that are specified for the purpose of subsection 111(2) in a notice published under subsection (1) or (3); or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: indicate that a class of persons is no longer specified for the purpose of subsection 111(2).

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, by notice published in the Canada Gazette, vary the significant new activities in relation to a living organism in respect of which a notice has been given under subsection (1) or indicate that subsection 106(4) no longer applies with respect to that living organism.

A notice referred to in subsection (1) or (2) shall indicate, by inclusion or exclusion, the significant new activities in relation to the living organism in respect of which subsection 106(4) is to apply, and where regulations in respect of those significant new activities are not made under paragraphs 114(1)(c), (d) and (g), specify the information to be provided to the Minister under that subsection, the date on or before which it is to be provided and the period within which it is to be assessed under section 108.

Section.Text: Section 111 of the Act is renumbered as subsection 111(1) and is amended by adding the following:

Subsection.Text: A person who is within a class of persons that is specified in a notice published under subsection 110(1) or (3) â or, if the class is varied by a notice published under subsection 110(4), within the class as varied â is not required to be notified under subsection (1) with respect to the living organism to which the notice relates.

Section.Text: Subsections 112(3) and (4) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If a living organism is on the Domestic Substances List or is to be added to the List under subsection (1) or 105.1(1), the Minister may amend the List to indicate that subsection 106(3) applies with respect to the living organism.
An amendment referred to in subsection (3)

Paragraph.Text: shall indicate, by inclusion or exclusion, the significant new activities in relation to the living organism with respect to which subsection 106(3) is to apply and, if regulations in respect of those significant new activities are not made under paragraphs 114(1)(c), (d) and (g), specify the information to be provided to the Minister under that subsection, the date on or before which it is to be provided and the period within which it is to be assessed under section 108; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: may specify for the purpose of subsection 112.1(2) classes of persons who are not required to be notified under subsection 112.1(1) with respect to the living organism.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may amend the Domestic Substances List in respect of a living organism with respect to which subsection 106(3) applies

Paragraph.Text: to vary the significant new activities in relation to the living organism or vary the information to be provided to the Minister under subsection 106(3), the date on or before which that information is to be provided or the period within which it is to be assessed under section 108;
to indicate that subsection 106(3) no longer applies;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: to specify classes of persons for the purpose of subsection 112.1(2), if no such classes are specified with respect to the living organism;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: to vary the classes of persons, if any, that are specified for the purpose of subsection 112.1(2); or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: to delete any class of persons that is specified for the purpose of subsection 112.1(2).

Subsection.Text: Where a living organism is on the Domestic Substances List or is to be added to the List under subsection (1), the Minister may amend the List in respect of the living organism to indicate that subsection 106(3) applies with respect to the living organism or that it no longer applies or by varying the significant new activities in relation to the living organism in respect of which subsection 106(3) is to apply.

An amendment referred to in subsection (3) shall indicate, by inclusion or exclusion, the significant new activities in relation to the living organism in respect of which subsection 106(3) is to apply, and where regulations in respect of those significant new activities are not made under paragraphs 114(1)(c), (d) and (g), specify the information to be provided to the Minister under that subsection, the date on or before which it is to be provided and the period within which it is to be assessed under section 108.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 112:

MarginalNote.Ins: Notification of persons required to comply

Subsection.Text: If a living organism is specified on the Domestic Substances List with an indication that subsection 106(3) applies with respect to the living organism, every person who transfers the physical possession or control of the living organism shall notify all persons to whom the possession or control is transferred of the obligation to comply with that subsection.

A person is not required to be notified under subsection (1) with respect to a living organism if they are within a class of persons that is specified on the Domestic Substances List in respect of the living organism for the purpose of this subsection.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 199(1)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a substance or group of substances specified in Part 1 or 2 of the list of toxic substances in Schedule 1; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a substance or group of substances in relation to which there has been published in the Canada Gazette

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a copy of an order proposed to be made under subsection 90(1).

Subsection.Text: The Minister may at any time publish in the Canada Gazette, and in any other manner that the Minister considers appropriate, a notice requiring any person or class of persons described in the notice to prepare and implement an environmental emergency plan respecting the prevention of, preparedness for, response to or recovery from an environmental emergency in respect of

Paragraph.Label: (a)

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a copy of an order proposed to be made under subsection 90(1).

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 209(2)(l) to (r) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: the total, partial or conditional prohibition of the manufacture, use, processing, sale, offering for sale, import or export of the substance, a product that contains the substance or a product that may release the substance into the environment and the total, partial or conditional prohibition of the manufacture, import or export of a product that is intended to contain the substance;

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: the quantity or concentration of the substance that may be contained in any product manufactured, imported, exported, sold or offered for sale in Canada, or that any such product may release into the environment;

Paragraph.Label: (n)
Paragraph.Text: the manner in which, the conditions under which and the purposes for which the substance, a product that contains the substance or a product that may release the substance into the environment may be advertised or offered for sale;

Paragraph.Label: (o)
Paragraph.Text: the manner in which and the conditions under which the substance, a product that contains the substance or a product that may release the substance into the environment may be stored, displayed, handled, transported or offered for transport;

Paragraph.Label: (p)
Paragraph.Text: the packaging and labelling of the substance, a product that contains the substance or a product that may release the substance into the environment;

Paragraph.Label: (q)
Paragraph.Text: the manner, conditions, places and method of disposal or recycling of the substance, a product that contains the substance or a product that may release the substance into the environment, including standards for the construction, maintenance and inspection of disposal or recycling sites;

Paragraph.Label: (r)
Paragraph.Text: the submission to the Minister, on request or at any times that are prescribed, of information relating to the substance, a product that contains the substance or a product that may release the substance into the environment;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 209(2)(t) and (u) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (t)
Paragraph.Text: the conduct of sampling, analyses, tests, measurements or monitoring of the substance, a product that contains the substance or a product that may release the substance into the environment and the submission of the results to the Minister;

Paragraph.Label: (u)
Paragraph.Text: the submission to the Minister of samples of the substance, a product that contains the substance or a product that may release the substance into the environment;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 209(2)(v) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (v)
Paragraph.Text: the conditions, test procedures and laboratory practices to be followed for conducting sampling, analyses, tests, measurements or monitoring of the substance, a product that contains the substance or a product that may release the substance into the environment;

Subsection.Text: Section 209 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

Regulations made under this section may authorize the Minister, in the circumstances and subject to the conditions and limits that may be specified in the regulations, to issue, amend, suspend and revoke permits and other authorizations in relation to any matter that is the subject of the regulations and to set their terms and conditions.
A permit or other authorization issued under regulations made under this section is not a statutory instrument as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Statutory Instruments Act.

Section.Text: Paragraph 218(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: there can be found in the place a substance with respect to which this Act applies, a product that contains such a substance or a product that may release such a substance into the environment;

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2), for the purposes of this Act and the regulations, an enforcement officer may, at any reasonable time, enter and inspect any place if the enforcement officer has reasonable grounds to believe that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: there can be found in the place a substance to which this Act applies or a product containing such a substance;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 235(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the exportation, importation, manufacture, transportation, processing or distribution of a substance, a product that contains a substance or a product that may release a substance into the environment;

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsection (1), the circumstances in which the alleged contravention has been or will be committed are as follows, namely,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the exportation, importation, manufacture, transportation, processing or distribution of a substance or product containing a substance;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the possession, storage, use, sale, offering for sale, advertisement or disposal of a substance or product containing a substance;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the use of a substance or product containing a substance in a commercial manufacturing or processing activity; or

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 235(2)(b) and (c) of the English version of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the possession, storage, use, sale, offering for sale, advertisement or disposal of a substance, a product that contains a substance or a product that may release a substance into the environment;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the use, in a commercial manufacturing or processing activity, of a substance, a product that contains a substance or a product that may release a substance into the environment; or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 235(3)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: owns or has the charge, management or control of

Subparagraph.Text: the substance â or any product that contains the substance or that may release the substance into the environment â to which the alleged contravention relates, or
the property on which the substance or product is located;

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to any person who

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: owns or has the charge, management or control of the substance or any product containing the substance to which the alleged contravention relates or the property on which the substance or product is located;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 272(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: fails to comply with an obligation set out in section 70, 86, 87.1, 95, 111 or 112.1 or subsection 169(1), 172(1), 179(1), 182(1), 201(1) or 212(1);

Subsection.Text: Every person commits an offence who

Paragraph.Text: Â

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: fails to comply with an obligation set out in section 70, 86, 95 or 111, subsection 169(1), 172(1), 179(1), 182(1), 201(1) or 212(1);
Â

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes a condition of a permission granted under paragraph 84(1)(a) or 109(1)(a);

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 272(1)(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes a condition of a permission granted under paragraph 84(1)(a) or 109(1)(a) or under regulations made under section 93 or 209;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 313(1) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Quiconque fournit des renseignements au ministre sous le rÃ©gime de la prÃ©sente loi, ou Ã la commission de rÃ©vision relativement Ã un avis dâopposition dÃ©posÃ© aux termes de la prÃ©sente loi, peut en mÃªme temps demander que les renseignements fournis soient considÃ©rÃ©s comme confidentiels.

A person who provides information to the Minister under this Act, or to a board of review in respect of a notice of objection filed under this Act, may submit with the information a request that it be treated as confidential.
A request under subsection (1) shall be submitted in writing and contain any supplementary information that may be prescribed.
Subsection 313(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:
A request for confidentiality shall be submitted, with reasons, in writing and contain any supplementary information that may be prescribed.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Exceptions to requirement to give reasons
Subsection.Text: In the case of information provided under a regulation, order or notice, reasons for a request for confidentiality are not required if the regulation, order or notice so specifies.

Section.Text: Section 314 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Minister shall not disclose any information in respect of which a request for confidentiality has been made under section 313, except in accordance with any of sections 315 to 317.2.
The Minister shall not disclose any information in respect of which a request for confidentiality has been made under section 313, except in accordance with section 315, 316 or 317.

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 316(1)(c) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: under an agreement or arrangement between the Government of Canada or any of its institutions and any other government in Canada, the government of a foreign state or an international organization or any of its institutions, or between the Minister and any other minister of the Crown in right of Canada or a board or agency of the Government of Canada, if

Subsection.Text: Information may be disclosed

Paragraph.Text: Â

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: under an agreement or arrangement between the Government of Canada or any of its institutions and any other government in Canada, the government of a foreign state or an international organization or any of its institutions, or between the Minister and any other minister of the Crown in right of Canada, where

Subparagraph.Text: Â

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the government, international organization, institution or other minister undertakes to keep the information confidential;

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 316(1)(c)(ii) of the Act of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the government, international organization, institution, other minister, board or agency undertakes to keep the information confidential;

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 317:

Subsection.Text: The Minister may disclose the explicit chemical or biological name of a substance in respect of which a request for confidentiality has been made if the Minister takes or has taken any of the following measures:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to permit, under paragraph 84(1)(a), the manufacture or importation of the substance, subject to any conditions that are specified;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to prohibit the manufacture or importation of the substance under paragraph 84(1)(b);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: to publish, in respect of the substance, a notice under subsection 85(1) or (4); or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: to amend the Domestic Substances List in respect of the substance under subsection 87(3) or (4.1).

Subsection.Text: The Minister may disclose the explicit biological name of a living organism in respect of which a request for confidentiality has been made if the Minister takes or has taken any of the following measures:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to permit, under paragraph 109(1)(a), the manufacture or importation of the living organism, subject to any conditions that are specified;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to prohibit the manufacture or importation of the living organism under paragraph 109(1)(b);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: to publish, in respect of the living organism, a notice under subsection 110(1) or (4); or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: to amend the Domestic Substances List in respect of the living organism under subsection 112(3) or (5).

Subsection.Text: The Minister may disclose the explicit chemical or biological name of a substance or the explicit biological name of a living organism in respect of which a request for confidentiality has been made if the Ministers recommend or have recommended to the Governor in Council the addition of the substance or the living organism to Part 1 or 2 of the list of toxic substances in Schedule 1 under subsection 90(1).

The Minister may disclose the explicit chemical or biological name of a substance or the explicit biological name of a living organism after the expiry of a period of 10 years after the day on which a request for confidentiality under section 313 is made.

The Minister shall publish a notice in the Canada Gazette of the Ministerâs intention to disclose the explicit chemical or biological name under subsection (1) at least 60 days before the proposed disclosure.
Within 60 days after the publication of the notice, the person who made the request for confidentiality or the personâs successor or assign may file with the Minister comments respecting

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: whether the public interest in the disclosure of the name clearly outweighs in importance

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: any material financial loss or prejudice to the competitive position of the person or the personâs successor or assign, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: any damage to the privacy, reputation or human dignity of any person that may result from the disclosure of that name; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: whether the disclosure is prohibited under section 20 of the Access to Information Act.

Subsection.Text: At least 24 hours before disclosing the name under subsection (1), the Minister shall give notice of the proposed disclosure to the person who made the request for confidentiality or the personâs successor or assign. The notice is not required if the person to whom it is to be given cannot be found after reasonable efforts have been made to do so.
If an emergency exists, subsections (2) and (3) do not apply and the notice of disclosure required in subsection (4) may be given later than the time provided for in that subsection.

Section.Text: Section 319 of the Act is renumbered as subsection 319(1) and is amended by adding the following:

Subsection.Text: The regulation, order or notice under which information is provided to the Minister or board of review may specify that the requirement to give reasons for a request for confidentiality under section 313 with respect to the information in question does not apply.

Section.Text: Subsections 330(3) and (3.1) of the Act are repealed.

Subsection.Text: Except as provided in subsection (3.1), a regulation under this Act applies throughout Canada.
A regulation made under section 93, 140, 167 or 177 may be made applicable in only a part or parts of Canada in order to protect the environment or its biological diversity or human health.

Section.Text: Subsections 332(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Publication of proposed orders and regulations

Subsection.Text: The Minister shall publish in the Canada Gazette a copy of every order or regulation proposed to be made by the Minister or the Governor in Council under this Act, except a list, or an amendment to a list, referred to in any of sections 66 to 66.2, 87, 105 to 105.2 and 112 or an interim order made under section 94, 163, 173, 183 or 200.1.

Any person may file with the Minister comments, or a notice of objection requesting that a board of review be established under section 333 and stating the reasons for the objection, within 60 days after the publication of

Paragraph.Text: a proposed order or regulation referred to in subsection (1);
a proposed instrument respecting preventive or control actions in relation to a substance that is required by section 91 to be published in the Canada Gazette; or

Text.Ins: a statement referred to in subparagraph 91(1)(a)(ii) or (b)(ii).

Subsection.Text: The Minister shall publish in the Canada Gazette a copy of every order or regulation proposed to be made by the Minister or the Governor in Council under this Act, except a list, or an amendment to a list, referred to in section 66, 87, 105 or 112 or an interim order made under section 94, 163, 173, 183 or 200.1.

Within 60 days after the publication of a proposed order or regulation in the Canada Gazette under subsection (1) or a proposed instrument respecting preventive or control actions in relation to a substance that is required by section 91 to be published in the Canada Gazette, any person may file with the Minister comments with respect to the order, regulation or instrument or a notice of objection requesting that a board of review be established under section 333 and stating the reasons for the objection.
Subsection 333(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If a person files a notice of objection under subsection 332(2) in respect of a decision or a proposed order, regulation or instrument made by the Governor in Council, a decision or a proposed order or instrument made by either or both Ministers or a statement referred to in subparagraph 91(1)(a)(ii) or (b)(ii), the Minister or the Ministers may establish a board of review to inquire into the nature and extent of the danger posed by the substance in respect of which the decision or statement is made or the order, regulation or instrument is proposed.
Where a person files a notice of objection under subsection 77(8) or 332(2) in respect of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a decision or a proposed order, regulation or instrument made by the Governor in Council, or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a decision or a proposed order or instrument made by either or both Ministers,

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: the Minister or the Ministers may establish a board of review to inquire into the nature and extent of the danger posed by the substance in respect of which the decision is made or the order, regulation or instrument is proposed.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 333(6) of the Act is repealed.

Where a person files with the Minister a notice of objection under section 78 in respect of the failure to make a determination about whether a substance is toxic, the Minister shall establish a board of review to inquire into whether the substance is toxic or capable of becoming toxic.

Section.Text: Schedule 1 to the Act is replaced by the Schedule 1 set out in the schedule to this Act.
Unless the context otherwise requires, words and expressions used in sections 60 to 62 have the same meaning as in the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999.

Section.MarginalNote: Statement under subsection 77(1) â deemed recommendation to add to Part 1

Subsection.Text: If, before the day on which subsection 21(1) of this Act comes into force, a statement is published under subsection 77(1) of the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 indicating that the measure that the Ministers propose to take in respect of a substance is a recommendation to add the substance to the List of Toxic Substances in Schedule 1 of that Act and to implement virtual elimination under subsection 65(3) of that Act and no statement confirming or amending that measure has been published under paragraph 77(6)(b) of that Act before that day, the measure is deemed to be a recommendation, made under paragraph 77(2)(c) of that Act, as that paragraph reads after that day, to add the substance on the list of toxic substances in Part 1 of Schedule 1, as that Schedule reads after that day.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Statement under subsection 77(1) â deemed recommendation to add to Part 2
Subsection.Text: If, before the day on which subsection 21(1) of this Act comes into force, a statement is published under subsection 77(1) of the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 indicating that the measure that the Ministers propose to take in respect of the substance is a recommendation to add the substance to the List of Toxic Substances in Schedule 1 of that Act without implementing virtual elimination under subsection 65(3) of that Act and no statement confirming or amending that measure is published under paragraph 77(6)(b) of that Act before that day, the measure is deemed to be a recommendation, made under paragraph 77(2)(d) of that Act, as enacted by that subsection 21(1), to add the substance on the list of toxic substances in Part 2 of Schedule 1, as that Schedule reads after that day.

Section.MarginalNote: Statement under paragraph 77(6)(b) â deemed recommendation to add to Part 1

Subsection.Text: If, before the day on which subsection 21(2) of this Act comes into force, a statement is published under paragraph 77(6)(b) of the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 indicating that the measure that the Ministers propose to take, as confirmed or amended, in respect of a substance is a recommendation to add the substance to the List of Toxic Substances in Schedule 1 of that Act and to implement virtual elimination under subsection 65(3) of that Act, the statement is deemed to be a statement, published under paragraph 77(6)(b) of that Act, as it reads after that day, indicating that the measure, as confirmed or amended, is a recommendation to add the substance on the list of toxic substances in Part 1 of Schedule 1, as that Schedule reads after that day.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Statement under paragraph 77(6)(b) â deemed recommendation to add to Part 2
Subsection.Text: If, before the day on which subsection 21(2) of this Act comes into force, a statement is published under paragraph 77(6)(b) of the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 indicating that the measure that the Ministers propose to take, as confirmed or amended, in respect of the substance is a recommendation to add the substance to the List of Toxic Substances in Schedule 1 of that Act without the implementation of virtual elimination under subsection 65(3) of that Act, the statement is deemed to be a statement, published under paragraph 77(6)(b) of that Act, as it reads after that day, indicating that the measure, as confirmed or amended, is a recommendation to add the substance on the list of toxic substances in Part 2 of Schedule 1, as that Schedule reads after that day.

If a substance is added to the List of Toxic Substances in Schedule 1 of the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 before the day on which section 58 of this Act comes into force but the substance is not specified on the list of toxic substances in Part 1 or 2 of Schedule 1 of the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 on that day, the Governor in Council must as soon as feasible after that day make an order adding the substance

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to the list of toxic substances in Part 1 of Schedule 1 of that Act, if the Governor in Council is satisfied that the substance is toxic within the meaning of section 64 of that Act and if the Ministers recommended the implementation of virtual elimination under subsection 65(3) of that Act in relation to the substance; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to the list of toxic substances in Part 2 of Schedule 1 of that Act, if the Governor in Council is satisfied that the substance is toxic within the meaning of section 64 of that Act and if the Ministers did not recommend the implementation of virtual elimination under subsection 65(3) of that Act in relation to the substance.

Subsection.Text: If a substance is deleted from the List of Toxic Substances in Schedule 1 of the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 before the day on which section 58 of this Act comes into force but the substance is specified on the list of toxic substances in Part 1 or 2 of Schedule 1 of that Act as it reads after that day, the Governor in Council must as soon as feasible after that day make an order deleting the substance from the list of toxic substances on which it is specified.

Section.Text: The Minister of the Environment and the Minister of Health may, by regulation,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: repeal the Virtual Elimination List; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: repeal the Regulations Adding Perfluorooctane Sulfonate and Its Salts to the Virtual Elimination List.

Heading.TitleText: Related Amendments to the Food and Drugs Act

Section.Text: The Food and Drugs Act is amended by adding the following after section 11:

It is prohibited for a person to sell, or to manufacture, prepare, preserve, package or store for sale, any drug that contains a prescribed substance, unless the Minister has conducted an assessment, in accordance with the regulations made under paragraph 30(1)(l.1), of the risk to the environment presented by the substance.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 21.3:

Subsection.Text: If the Minister believes that a therapeutic product may present a serious risk to the environment, the Minister may order a person to provide the Minister with information that is in the personâs control and that the Minister believes is necessary to determine whether the product presents such a risk.

The Minister may disclose confidential business information about a therapeutic product without notifying the person to whose business or affairs the information relates or obtaining their consent, if the Minister believes that the product may present a serious risk to the environment.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Disclosure â âprotection of the environment
Subsection.Text: The Minister may disclose confidential business information about a therapeutic product without notifying the person to whose business or affairs the information relates or obtaining their consent, if the purpose of the disclosure is related to the protection of the environment and the disclosure is to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a government;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a person from whom the Minister seeks advice; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a person who carries out functions relating to the protection of the environment, including the assessment and management of risks to the environment.

Subsection.Text: In this section, government has the same meaning as in subsection 21.1(4).

Section.MarginalNote: Labelling or packaging â serious risk to environment
Section.Text: The Minister may, if he or she believes that doing so is necessary to prevent a serious risk to the environment, order the holder of a therapeutic product authorization that authorizes the import or sale of a therapeutic product to modify the productâs label or to modify or replace its package.

Section.MarginalNote: Ministerâs powers â serious risk to environment

Subsection.Text: If the Minister believes that a therapeutic product presents a serious or imminent risk to the environment, he or she may order a person who sells the product to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: recall the product; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: send the product, or cause it to be sent, to a place specified in the order.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 21.3(2) to (6) apply, with any modifications that the circumstances require, in respect of any order made under subsection (1).

Section.Text: Section 21.4 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Power â information relating to environmental effects
Section.Text: Subject to the regulations, the Minister may, for the purpose of obtaining additional information about a therapeutic productâs effects on the environment, order the holder of a therapeutic product authorization to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: compile information or conduct tests, studies or monitoring in respect of the therapeutic product; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: provide the Minister with the information or the results of the tests, studies or monitoring.

Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, orders made under any of sections 21.1 to 21.33 are not statutory instruments within the meaning of the Statutory Instruments Act.
The Minister shall ensure that any order made under any of sections 21.1 to 21.33 is publicly available.
For greater certainty, orders made under any of sections 21.1 to 21.32 are not statutory instruments within the meaning of the Statutory Instruments Act.
The Minister shall ensure that any order made under any of sections 21.1 to 21.32 is publicly available.
Subsection 30(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a):

Paragraph.Label: (a.01)
Paragraph.Text: declaring that any drug is adulterated if the Minister believes that a prescribed substance contained in that drug presents a serious risk to the environment;

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations for carrying the purposes and provisions of this Act into effect, and, in particular, but without restricting the generality of the foregoing, may make regulations

Paragraph.Text: Â

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the method of manufacture, preparation, preserving, packing, storing and testing of any food, drug, cosmetic or device in the interest of, or for the prevention of injury to, the health of the purchaser or consumer;
Â

Paragraph.Label: (l.1)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the assessment of the effect on the environment or on human life and health of the release into the environment of any food, drug, cosmetic or device, and the measures to take before importing or selling any such food, drug, cosmetic or device;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 30(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a.01):

Paragraph.Label: (a.02)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing the substances that are subject to an assessment under section 11.1;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 30(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (b.01)
Paragraph.Text: respecting, for the purposes of managing risks to the environment, any of the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the labelling and packaging and the offering, exposing and advertising for sale of food, drugs, cosmetics and devices,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the size, dimensions, fill and other specifications of packages of food, drugs, cosmetics and devices,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the sale or the conditions of sale of any food, drug, cosmetic or device, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the use of any substance as an ingredient in any food, drug, cosmetic or device;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 30(1)(e) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the method of manufacture, preparation, preserving, packaging, storing and testing of any food, drug, cosmetic or device in the interest of, or for the prevention of injury to, the health of the purchaser or consumer or to manage risks to the environment;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 30(1)(l.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (l.1)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the assessment of the risk to the environment or human life and health of the release into the environment of any food, drug, cosmetic or device;

Paragraph.Label: (l.2)
Paragraph.Text: respecting, for the purpose of managing risks to the environment identified as part of an assessment under this Act, measures in respect of selling or importing, manufacturing, preparing, preserving, packaging or storing for sale any food, drug, cosmetic or device;

Paragraph.Label: (l.3)
Paragraph.Text: authorizing the Minister to waive any requirement respecting the assessment under this Act of the risk to the environment presented by a food, drug, cosmetic or device;

Subsection.Text: Section 30 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

Before recommending to the Governor in Council that a regulation be made under paragraph (1)(a.02), the Minister must take into account the degree of uncertainty respecting the risks to the environment associated with the use of a substance as an ingredient in a drug, including its release into the environment.
Subsection 30(1.2) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (d):

Paragraph.Label: (d.01)
Paragraph.Text: requiring holders of a therapeutic product authorization to provide the Minister with information, in respect of any serious risk to the environment, that the holders receive or become aware of and that is relevant to the therapeutic product to which the authorization relates, regarding

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: risks that have been communicated outside Canada, and the manner of the communication,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: changes that have taken place to labelling outside Canada, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: recalls, reassessments and suspensions or revocations of authorizations, including licences, in respect of a therapeutic product, that have taken place outside Canada;

Subsection.Text: Without limiting the power conferred by any other subsection of this section, the Governor in Council may make regulations

Paragraph.Text: Â

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: respecting modifications of labels and modifications and replacements of packages referred to in section 21.2;
Â

Paragraph.Label: (f.3)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the compilation of information, the conducting of tests and studies and the monitoring of experience that are referred to in paragraph 21.32(a), and the provision to the Minister of the information or results referred to in paragraph 21.32(b);

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 30(1.2)(e) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: respecting modifications of labels and modifications and replacements of packages referred to in sections 21.2 and 21.302;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 30(1.2)(f.3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (f.3)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the compilation of information, the conducting of tests and studies and the monitoring that are referred to in paragraphs 21.32(a) and 21.33(a), and the provision to the Minister of the information or results referred to in paragraphs 21.32(b) and 21.33(b);

Section.Text: The Perfluorooctane Sulfonate Virtual Elimination Act, chapter 13 of the Statutes of Canada, 2008, is repealed.
Section 64 and subsections 67(2), (5) and (6) come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

The following petroleum and refinery gases:

tail gas (petroleum), catalytic polymerized naphtha fractionation stabilizer (a complex combination of hydrocarbons â obtained from the fractionation stabilization products that result from the polymerization of naphtha â consisting predominantly of hydrocarbons having carbon numbers in the range of C1 through C4);
fuel gases (a combination of light gases consisting predominantly of hydrogen or low molecular weight hydrocarbons or both);

hydrocarbons, C2-C4, C3-rich (a complex combination of hydrocarbons â obtained from a treating process to remove sulphur and other acidic compounds â consisting of hydrocarbons having carbon numbers in the range of C2 through C4, predominantly propane and propene);

gases (petroleum), butane splitter overhead (a complex combination of hydrocarbons â obtained from the distillation of the butane stream â consisting of aliphatic hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C3 through C4);

gases (petroleum), catalytic cracked gas oil depropanizer bottom, C4-rich acid-free (a complex combination of hydrocarbons â obtained from the fractionation of catalytic cracked gas oil hydrocarbon stream and treated to remove hydrogen sulfide and other acidic components â consisting of hydrocarbons having carbon numbers in the range of C3 through C5, predominantly C4);

gases (petroleum), catalytic cracked naphtha debutanizer bottom, C3-C5-rich (a complex combination of hydrocarbons â obtained from the stabilization of catalytic cracked naphtha â consisting of aliphatic hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C3 through C5);

gases (petroleum), catalytic cracked naphtha depropanizer overhead, C3-rich acid-free (a complex combination of hydrocarbons â obtained from the fractionation of catalytic cracked hydrocarbons and treated to remove acidic impurities â consisting of hydrocarbons having carbon numbers in the range of C2 through C4, predominantly C3);

gases (petroleum), catalytic cracker, C1-C5-rich (a complex combination of hydrocarbons â obtained from the distillation of products that result from a catalytic cracking process â consisting of aliphatic hydrocarbons having carbon numbers in the range of C1 through C6, predominantly C1 through C5);

gases (petroleum), catalytic polymerized naphtha stabilizer overhead, C2-C4-rich (a complex combination of hydrocarbons â obtained from the fractionation stabilization of catalytic polymerized naphtha â consisting of aliphatic hydrocarbons having carbon numbers in the range of C2 through C6, predominantly C2 through C4);

gases (petroleum), catalytic reformed naphtha stripper overhead (a complex combination of hydrocarbons â obtained from the stabilization of catalytic reformed naphtha â consisting of hydrogen and saturated aliphatic hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C1 through C4);

gases (petroleum), deethanizer overhead (a complex combination of hydrocarbons â obtained from the distillation of the gas and gasoline fractions that result from a catalytic cracking process â consisting predominantly of ethane and ethene);

gases (petroleum), deisobutanizer tower overhead (a complex combination of hydrocarbons â obtained from the atmospheric distillation of a butane-butene stream â consisting of aliphatic hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C3 through C4);

gases (petroleum), gas concentration reabsorber distillation (a complex combination of hydrocarbons â obtained from the distillation of products from combined gas streams in a gas concentration reabsorber â consisting predominantly of hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, nitrogen, hydrogen sulfide and hydrocarbons having carbon numbers in the range of C1 through C3);

gases (petroleum), hydrogen-rich (a complex combination â separated as a gas from hydrocarbon gases by chilling â consisting predominantly of hydrogen with small amounts of carbon monoxide, nitrogen, methane and C2 hydrocarbons);

gases (petroleum), recycle, hydrogen-rich (a complex combination â obtained from recycled reactor gases â consisting predominantly of hydrogen with small amounts of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, nitrogen, hydrogen sulfide and saturated aliphatic hydrocarbons having carbon numbers in the range of C1 through C5);

gases (petroleum), reformer make-up, hydrogen-rich (a complex combination â obtained from the reformers â consisting predominantly of hydrogen with small amounts of carbon monoxide and aliphatic hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C1 through C5);

gases (petroleum), thermal cracking distillation (a complex combination â obtained from the distillation of products that result from a thermal cracking process â consisting of hydrogen, hydrogen sulfide, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C1 through C6);

tail gas (petroleum), catalytic cracker refractionation absorber (a complex combination of hydrocarbons â obtained from the refractionation of products that result from a catalytic cracking process â consisting of hydrogen and hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C1 through C3);

tail gas (petroleum), cracked distillate hydrotreater separator (a complex combination of hydrocarbons â obtained by treating cracked distillates with hydrogen in the presence of a catalyst â consisting of hydrogen and saturated aliphatic hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C1 through C5);

tail gas (petroleum), saturate gas plant mixed stream, C4-rich (a complex combination of hydrocarbons â obtained from the fractionation stabilization of straight-run naphtha, distillation tail gas and catalytic reformed naphtha stabilizer tail gas â consisting of hydrocarbons having carbon numbers in the range of C3 through C6, predominantly butane and isobutane);

tail gas (petroleum), vacuum residue thermal cracker (a complex combination of hydrocarbons â obtained from the thermal cracking of vacuum residues â consisting of hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C1 through C5);

hydrocarbons, C3-C4-rich, petroleum distillates (a complex combination of hydrocarbons â obtained from the distillation and condensation of crude oil â consisting of hydrocarbons having carbon numbers in the range of C3 through C5, predominantly C3 and C4);

gases (petroleum), hydrocracking depropanizer off, hydrocarbon-rich (a complex combination of hydrocarbons â obtained from the distillation of products that result from a hydrocracking process â consisting predominantly of hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C1 through C4);

gases (petroleum), light straight-run naphtha stabilizer off (a complex combination of hydrocarbons â obtained from the stabilization of light straight-run naphtha â consisting of saturated aliphatic hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C2 through C6);

gases (petroleum), reformer effluent high-pressure flash drum off (a complex combination â obtained from the high-pressure flashing of the effluent from the reforming reactor â consisting predominantly of hydrogen with small amounts of methane, ethane and propane);

hydrocarbons, C1-C4 (a complex combination of hydrocarbons â obtained from thermal cracking and absorber operations and from the distillation of crude oil â consisting of hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C1 through C4 and boiling in the range of approximately -164Â°C to -0.5Â°C);

hydrocarbons, C1-C4, sweetened (a complex combination of hydrocarbons â obtained by subjecting hydrocarbon gases to a sweetening process to convert mercaptans or to remove acidic impurities â consisting of hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C1 through C4 and boiling in the range of approximately -164Â°C to -0.5Â°C);
hydrocarbons, C1-C3 (a complex combination of hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C1 through C3 and boiling in the range of approximately -164Â°C to -42Â°C);

gases (petroleum), C1-C5, wet (a complex combination of hydrocarbons â obtained from the distillation of crude oil or the cracking of tower gas oil or both â consisting of hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C1 through C5);

gases (petroleum), secondary absorber off, fluidized catalytic cracker overhead fractionater (a complex combination â obtained from the fractionation of the overhead products that result from a catalytic cracking process in the fluidized catalytic cracker â consisting of hydrogen, nitrogen and hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C1 through C3);

gases (petroleum), alkylation feed (a complex combination of hydrocarbons â obtained from the catalytic cracking of gas oil â consisting of hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C3 through C4);
petroleum products, refinery gases (a complex combination consisting predominantly of hydrogen with small amounts of methane, ethane and propane);

gases (petroleum), refinery (a complex combination â obtained from various petroleum refining operations â consisting of hydrogen and hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C1 through C3);

gases (petroleum), hydrotreated sour kerosine depentanizer stabilizer off (a complex combination â obtained from the depentanizer stabilization of hydrotreated kerosine â consisting predominantly of hydrogen, methane, ethane and propane with small amounts of nitrogen, hydrogen sulfide, carbon monoxide and hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C4 through C5);

gases (petroleum), crude oil fractionation off (a complex combination of hydrocarbons â obtained from the fractionation of crude oil â consisting of saturated aliphatic hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C1 through C5);

gases (petroleum), fluidized catalytic cracker fractionation off (a complex combination â obtained from the fractionation of the overhead products that result from a fluidized catalytic cracking process â consisting of hydrogen, hydrogen sulfide, nitrogen and hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C1 through C5);

gases (petroleum), heavy distillate hydrotreater desulfurization stripper off (a complex combination â stripped from the liquid product that results from a heavy distillate hydrotreater desulfurization process â consisting of hydrogen, hydrogen sulfide and saturated aliphatic hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C1 through C5);

gases (petroleum), preflash tower off, crude distillation (a complex combination â produced from the first tower used in the distillation of crude oil â consisting of nitrogen and saturated aliphatic hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C1 through C5);

gases (petroleum), straight-run stabilizer off (a complex combination of hydrocarbons â obtained from the fractionation of the liquid produced from the first tower used in the distillation of crude oil â consisting of saturated aliphatic hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C1 through C4);

tail gas (petroleum), catalytic hydrodesulfurized naphtha separator (a complex combination of hydrocarbons â obtained from the catalytic hydrodesulfurization of naphtha â consisting of hydrogen, methane, ethane and propane);

gases (petroleum), C3-C4 (a complex combination of hydrocarbons â obtained from the distillation of products that result from the cracking of crude oil â consisting of hydrocarbons having carbon numbers in the range of C3 through C4, predominantly propane and propene, and boiling in the range of approximately -51Â°C to -1Â°C);

gases (petroleum), C3-C4, isobutane-rich (a complex combination of hydrocarbons â obtained from the distillation of saturated and unsaturated hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C3 through C6, predominantly butane and isobutane â consisting of saturated and unsaturated hydrocarbons having carbon numbers in the range of C3 through C4, predominantly isobutane);

gases (petroleum), C4-rich (a complex combination of hydrocarbons â obtained from the distillation of products that result from a catalytic fractionation process â consisting of aliphatic hydrocarbons having carbon numbers in the range of C3 through C5, predominantly C4);
hydrocarbons, C1-C4, debutanizer fraction (a complex combination of hydrocarbons â obtained from a debutanizing process â having carbon numbers in the range of C1 through C4);

petroleum gases, liquefied (a complex combination of hydrocarbons â obtained from the distillation of crude oil â consisting of hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C3 through C7 and boiling in the range of approximately -40Â°C to 80Â°C); and

petroleum gases, liquefied, sweetened (a complex combination of hydrocarbons â obtained by subjecting liquefied petroleum gases to a sweetening process to convert mercaptans or to remove acidic impurities â consisting of hydrocarbons having carbon numbers predominantly in the range of C3 through C7 and boiling in the range of approximately -40Â°C to 80Â°C).
